message:
fix: harden localStorage parsing and Suspense focus preservation

Two defensive improvements to prevent runtime errors and UX regressions:

1. store.ts - Type-safe localStorage hydration:
   - beginnerModeEnabled: Only accept boolean values from localStorage,
     reject malformed data that could cause type corruption
   - completedTours: Filter stored array to strings only before merging
   - completedModules: Same string-only filtering applied

   This prevents scenarios where corrupted localStorage (e.g., from
   browser extensions, manual tampering, or serialization bugs) could
   inject non-string values into arrays or non-boolean into flags,
   causing downstream TypeScript assumptions to fail at runtime.

2. Overlay.tsx - Preserve focus during Suspense boundary swaps:
   - Previously: PREVIOUS_FOCUS_BY_OVERLAY_ID.delete() ran on every unmount
   - Problem: During Suspense fallback swaps, the overlay unmounts briefly
     while still logically "open", losing the stored focus target
   - Fix: Only delete focus entry if overlayIsOpenLatestRef.current is false

   This ensures that when React swaps Suspense boundaries (e.g., lazy
   loading content inside an overlay), the replacement component can
   still restore focus correctly when the overlay finally closes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
